title: Fix canonical repository identity

sources.reason: Records the review findings and remediation backlog.
Defines the release distribution source identity.
Catches stale repository identity in documentation.

problemStatement: The repository has migrated to spirex-ds-dev/ai-cockpit-template, but installer, release-distribution checks, tests, and documentation still default to or assert the former xinglun namespace. Redirects are not a trustworthy supply-chain identity.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: default installer source resolves to the canonical repository
release distribution check rejects a non-canonical repository identity
explicit repository override remains available to adopters

riskAssessment.reason: Changing defaults can affect existing adopters that explicitly rely on the old source; compatibility is preserved through explicit override support while stale defaults are rejected.

executionDecision.reason: The canonical repository identity and compatibility boundary are explicit and testable.

preReviewWarnings: Review that every default, URL, generated instruction, and regression fixture uses the canonical repository while explicit override behavior remains documented.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: All default distribution sources use spirex-ds-dev/ai-cockpit-template.
Release-distribution checks validate canonical owner/name and installer output reports the resolved repository identity.
Tests fail for stale xinglun defaults and pass for the canonical identity, including explicit override behavior.
Documentation and generated installation examples use the canonical repository.
No release tag or version number is changed; release.json digests are synchronized only with the candidate evidence baselines.
guidelines: Evidence over self-declaration: identity is checked from executable defaults and tests.
Keep explicit repository override support for adopters while making the canonical source the default.
Do not change release version or publish assets in this Work Item.

intent.problem: The review found that the repository's distribution identity still points at the former GitHub namespace, so a redirect rather than an exact canonical source is trusted during installation and release checks.
constraints: Use spirex-ds-dev/ai-cockpit-template as the canonical default.
Preserve explicit override support for adopter repositories.
Do not publish or mutate a release version in this Work Item.
intent.rationale: Canonical identity is a prerequisite for trustworthy release evidence and must be corrected before provenance and release validation can be trusted.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly confirmed execution of the trust remediation loop; candidate supply-chain evidence must be regenerated to validate the Provenance fix.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
